name: Linear Equations
description: Systems of linear equations and their solutions using substitution, elimination, and matrix methods. Foundation of all linear algebra concepts.
subject: Mathematics I

name: Matrix Operations
description: Addition, multiplication, transpose, and inverse of matrices. Essential for representing and solving systems of equations.
subject: Mathematics I

name: Determinants
description: Computing determinants using cofactor expansion and row reduction. Used to check matrix invertibility.
subject: Mathematics I

name: Vector Spaces
description: Vector spaces, subspaces, span, and linear independence. Abstract framework for linear algebra.
subject: Mathematics I

name: Single Variable Calculus
description: Limits, derivatives, and integrals of single-variable functions. Building block for multivariable calculus.
subject: Mathematics I

name: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
description: Finding eigenvalues and eigenvectors of matrices. Central to PCA, spectral methods, and stability analysis.
subject: Mathematics II

name: Multivariable Calculus
description: Partial derivatives, gradients, Jacobians, and Hessians. Required for optimization and machine learning.
subject: Mathematics II

name: Probability Fundamentals
description: Probability axioms, conditional probability, Bayes theorem, and common distributions.
subject: Mathematics II

name: Statistical Inference
description: Hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, maximum likelihood estimation. Foundation for ML evaluation.
subject: Mathematics II

name: Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Solving first and second order ODEs. Appears in control systems and dynamical models.
subject: Mathematics II

name: Lagrange Multipliers
description: Method for constrained optimization using Lagrangian functions. Core to SVM dual formulation and many ML problems.
subject: Mathematics III

name: Convex Optimization
description: Convex sets, convex functions, and optimization algorithms for convex problems. Guarantees global optimum.
subject: Mathematics III

name: Linear Transformations
description: Mappings between vector spaces that preserve vector addition and scalar multiplication.
subject: Mathematics III

name: Numerical Methods
description: Iterative algorithms for root finding, interpolation, and numerical integration. Practical computation skills.
subject: Mathematics III

name: Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues
description: Fundamental data structures with constant and linear time operations. Every algorithm builds on these.
subject: Data Structures

name: Trees and Graphs
description: Binary trees, BSTs, graph representation, BFS, DFS. Core to many search and traversal problems.
subject: Data Structures

name: Hash Tables
description: Hash functions, collision resolution, and amortized O(1) lookup. Used extensively in practice.
subject: Data Structures

name: Sorting and Searching Algorithms
description: Merge sort, quicksort, binary search, and their time complexity analysis.
subject: Algorithms

name: Dynamic Programming
description: Solving optimization problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems. Key technique for competitive programming.
subject: Algorithms

name: Greedy Algorithms
description: Making locally optimal choices at each step. Works for specific problem structures like MST and Huffman coding.
subject: Algorithms

name: Graph Algorithms
description: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Floyd-Warshall, topological sort. Essential for network and path problems.
subject: Algorithms

name: Time and Space Complexity
description: Big-O notation, worst case analysis, NP-completeness basics. Framework for evaluating algorithm efficiency.
subject: Algorithms

name: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and design patterns. Standard paradigm for large codebases.
subject: Object-Oriented Design

name: Relational Database Fundamentals
description: ER diagrams, normalization, SQL queries, and ACID properties. Every backend system uses databases.
subject: DBMS

name: Operating System Concepts
description: Process scheduling, memory management, file systems, and concurrency. Foundation for systems programming.
subject: Operating Systems

name: Linear Regression
description: Fitting a linear model to data using least squares. The simplest and most interpretable ML model.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Logistic Regression
description: Binary classification using sigmoid function and cross-entropy loss. Gateway to understanding neural networks.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Gradient Descent
description: Iterative optimization by moving in the direction of steepest descent. The engine behind most ML training.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Regularization (L1/L2)
description: Preventing overfitting by adding penalty terms to the loss function. L1 for sparsity, L2 for smoothness.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
description: Understanding model complexity through the lens of underfitting vs overfitting. Central to model selection.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Decision Trees
description: Tree-based classification using information gain or Gini impurity. Interpretable and fast to train.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Ensemble Methods (Random Forest, Boosting)
description: Combining multiple weak learners for stronger predictions. Random Forest for variance, Boosting for bias.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Cross-Validation
description: K-fold and stratified validation techniques for reliable model evaluation without data leakage.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Kernel Methods
description: The kernel trick maps data to higher dimensions without explicit computation. Enables non-linear SVMs.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Support Vector Machines
description: Maximum margin classifier using support vectors. Formulated as a constrained optimization problem with Lagrangian duality.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Principal Component Analysis
description: Dimensionality reduction by projecting data onto eigenvectors of the covariance matrix.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Clustering (K-Means, DBSCAN)
description: Unsupervised grouping of data points. K-Means minimizes within-cluster variance, DBSCAN finds density-based clusters.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Neural Networks
description: Multilayer perceptrons with backpropagation. Universal function approximators when given enough capacity.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Backpropagation
description: Computing gradients through a computation graph using the chain rule. The core training algorithm for neural networks.
subject: Machine Learning

name: Convolutional Neural Networks
description: Neural networks with convolutional layers for spatial feature extraction. Dominant in image recognition.
subject: Deep Learning

name: Recurrent Neural Networks and LSTM
description: Sequential models with memory. LSTM gates solve the vanishing gradient problem for long sequences.
subject: Deep Learning

name: Attention Mechanism
description: Weighted focus on relevant parts of input sequences. Foundation of the Transformer architecture.
subject: Deep Learning

name: Transformer Architecture
description: Self-attention based architecture that replaced RNNs for sequence tasks. Powers all modern language models.
subject: Deep Learning

name: Transfer Learning
description: Using pre-trained models on new tasks. Fine-tuning saves compute and improves performance on small datasets.
subject: Deep Learning

name: Word Embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe)
description: Dense vector representations of words that capture semantic relationships. Foundation for NLP.
subject: NLP

name: Text Classification
description: Assigning categories to text using bag-of-words, TF-IDF, or neural methods. Spam detection, sentiment analysis.
subject: NLP

name: Generative Adversarial Networks
description: Two-network architecture where a generator and discriminator compete. Used for image synthesis and data augmentation.
subject: Advanced ML

name: Reinforcement Learning Basics
description: Agent learns by interacting with an environment to maximize cumulative reward. Q-learning and policy gradient methods.
subject: Advanced ML

name: Graph Neural Networks
description: Neural networks that operate on graph-structured data. Message passing between nodes for node and graph classification.
subject: Advanced ML
